name: Claude Agent SDK
analogy: Like hiring a team of specialist workers — each one knows how to do one job really well, and a manager coordinates them to get the whole project done.
whatItDoes: Lets you build custom AI agents that can work independently on tasks. Instead of one AI doing everything, you can create multiple agents — each with specific tools and instructions — that work together. Think of it as giving Claude Code the ability to send out mini versions of itself to handle different parts of a job.
whyYouUseIt: Claude Code already uses sub-agents behind the scenes — that's how it builds your projects so fast (like DevToolkit, where it dispatched separate agents to build each page in parallel). If you want to build your own AI-powered tools for clients, this SDK is how you'd do it.
whenToSwap: If a client specifically wants OpenAI instead of Claude, use OpenAI Agents SDK. If you need a framework with lots of pre-built integrations and community examples, LangChain has a bigger ecosystem. For simple single-agent tasks, you don't need an SDK at all — just the regular Claude API.
